title: The companion mod with live spectating, content brackets, draft synergies, API keys with tiered rate limits, Patreon supporters, and a wiki redesign
features: The Spire Codex companion mod is live on the Steam Workshop (also on Nexus Mods). It tracks your runs as you play and powers everything new in this release: automatic run uploads, live presence, and real-time spectating. (PRs #444, #453, #487, #502)

Live spectating: the new /live page shows who is playing with the mod right now, and every live player gets a spectator page you can open and watch. The spectator view renders a game-like battle scene with enemy portraits, room backgrounds, and pets, plus an act map with progressive boss and ancient reveals, a play-by-play event ticker, live loot, shop, and event panels, and click-to-open deck and pile drawers with full card renders. The home page has a Live now rail and the navbar has a Live players button. (PRs #463 through #471, #537 through #552, #557 through #564)
Twitch on /live: connect a Twitch account in Settings and a Watch on Twitch button appears on your live card while you stream. (PRs #512, #514)

API keys: every account can create one API key under Settings, in the new API Key tab. Keys use the sk-codex- prefix, are shown once at creation, and carry a usage counter (today and last 7 days). The /developers page documents the program with a live rate-limit table. (PRs #632 through #636)

Tiered rate limits: general traffic, registered keys, academia, and paid tiers each get their own per-endpoint caps, counted exactly across all backend workers on Redis. Every API response now carries X-RateLimit headers so clients can pace themselves. Limits are tunable at runtime from the admin panel. (PRs #631, #635, #637)

Patreon: link a Patreon account in Settings to get a Supporter badge, and active patrons are bumped to the paid API tier automatically, including live sync when a pledge starts or ends. Patreon also joined the navbar Support group. (PRs #639, #642, #643)

Content brackets across the stats: filter the tier lists, community stats, and entity pages by skill bracket (All, A10, or top 30/50/75 percent win rate) and by player count (Solo, 2P, 3P, 4P), and combine the two axes freely. (PRs #521, #570, #575, #585)

Played by character: the metrics leaderboard can now filter card, relic, and potion rows by which character actually picked them, on top of any bracket. This came straight from community feedback. (PR #644)

Codex Elo: the metrics pages gained a head-to-head Elo rating computed from draft picks, including upgrade Elo for + cards, an Elo tier view, and weekly trend charts on every entity page that archive Codex Score and Elo over time for every bracket. (PRs #414 through #418, #592, #593, #628 through #630)

Draft synergies: card, relic, and potion pages show an Often drafted with panel built from co-occurrence in winning decks, with starters excluded so real synergies surface. On top of that sits an offer-conditioned draft recommender: given your deck and an offer, it ranks the choices by how similar winning decks drafted. (PRs #616 through #620)

Entity pages got deeper: cards list which monsters generate them in a Sources section, curated and derived trivia, encounter pages carry a community deadliness line, energy costs render as proper tokens, hovertips show entity art, and Other languages plus Version history sections round out each page. (PRs #605, #606, #607, #622, #624)

Site redesign: the whole site moved to a Catppuccin Latte wiki-style layout with the Kreon typeface, a single announcement ticker instead of stacked banners, a reordered homepage character bar, and a dark theme pass that lifted secondary text into a readable contrast range. (PRs #486, #587, #589, #590, #610, #612)
Global search now covers the whole site: every entity type, pages, and mechanics from one search bar. (PR #430)
/charts: an interactive run-data explorer on Chart.js with A10, win-rate, mods, and version filters, prewarmed so it loads fast. (PRs #439, #440, #489, #491)
Community stats got its own page with interactive charts, a beta content spotlight, and mini-stats that follow whichever bracket you have selected. (PRs #420 through #423, #454, #600)
Tier list maker: build and share your own tier lists for cards, relics, potions, and monsters, with act and ancient filters and beta content included. (PRs #406 through #408, #425, #434, #436, #449)

The beta site merged into spire-codex.com/beta on the main domain, and the beta channel ingested game versions v0.107.0, v0.107.1, and v0.108.0 with art, renders, and changelogs. (PRs #409, #448, #498, #560)

Stats data quality: public stats now count only official content and official ascensions (A0 to A10), modded relics and characters are filtered out, tokens no longer get per-card rows, and admins can hide cheated runs from the rankings. (PRs #505, #506, #515, #551, #568, #571, #574)
SEO and sharing: entity titles lead with the name, and the community data unique to Spire Codex (scores, win rates, deadliness) is server-rendered so it shows up in search results. (PR #594)
A /giveaway page ran the Slay the Spire 2 shadowbox giveaway. (PR #520)

Under the hood: the entity-stats snapshot rebuild can now fan out across CPU cores, stats keep serving through deploys and version bumps, workers no longer re-walk every run file at startup, and releases stopped re-uploading a 2.6 GB static layer every deploy. (PRs #431, #450, #458, #461, #586)
fixes: Hotfixed a bad rate-limit callable that briefly 500ed every API request during the tiered-limits rollout, and added full-stack middleware tests so it cannot recur. (PR #638)
API key and admin responses are never edge-cached anymore; a cached empty key list could previously mask a freshly created key. (PR #640)
CDN images no longer break on Firefox (especially mobile): every image now loads in CORS mode so a cache entry poisoned by a mixed-mode load cannot blank out character art. (PR #641)
Co-op-only cards (like Demonic Shield) no longer appear in Solo stats. (PR #644)
Monster multi-hit intents display correctly (Aeonglass Eye Lasers is 12x2, not 12). (PRs #601, #603)
Single-target cards no longer render as AoE when their target type is choose. (PR #435)
The potion drop-rate page had the pity mechanic backwards: a bonus-zone drop lowers pity, it does not raise it. (PR #516)

A site-wide correctness sweep against the decompiled game code fixed a batch of wrong numbers and descriptions, and the scoring page now notes that reward screens can offer upgraded cards. (PRs #517, #569)
Ancient relic pools are now generated straight from the game code instead of maintained by hand, which fixed the v0.107.1 pool drift (Neow's new relics, Tezcatara's Seal of Gold). (PRs #500, #501)
Steam linking no longer creates duplicate accounts, and admin rename and merge bust the run-detail cache. (PRs #510, #511)
Encounter deadliness no longer picks tiny noise rows (Bygone Effigy showed stats from 2 runs). (PR #608)

Mobile fixes: the home stats table and Recent Runs box no longer scroll sideways, the live pill no longer pushes the profile menu off-screen, and the run upload drop zone buttons are readable again. (PRs #480, #590, #613, #625)
The beta /api/cards endpoint no longer 500s on cards with fractional variables, and beta-only entities resolve their images on run pages and tier lists. (PRs #472, #483, #492, #572)
Restored missing or doubled power icons (Withering Presence, Tainted, live combat powers). (PRs #552, #573)
The runs export no longer 500s on legacy timestamps, and /charts survives post-deploy cold starts. (PRs #567, #596)
api_changes: New /api/keys endpoints to create, list, and revoke an API key (one per account), authenticated via the X-API-Key header. /api/rate-limits returns the live per-tier, per-endpoint caps, and every response now carries X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers.
New /api/auth/patreon endpoints for linking a Patreon account, plus a webhook that syncs pledge changes to the paid tier.

New /api/presence endpoints power live tracking: mods POST presence beats, /api/presence/active lists live players, and /api/presence/{steam_id} returns one player's live state for the spectator pages.
New /api/pairings endpoints return item co-occurrence synergies, and POST /api/draft-advice ranks an offer against a partial deck.
/api/runs/metrics/{entity_type} gained bracket and character parameters, and /api/runs/stats accepts combined player-count and skill brackets (for example 2p_wr50) everywhere brackets apply.
New /api/runs/stats/{entity_type}/{entity_id}/history returns the weekly Codex Score and Elo archive behind the trend charts.
/api/runs/encounter-stats gained a game-version axis, and the runs export gained keyset pagination and time-range filters.
Admin, feedback, auth, and telemetry endpoints are hidden from the public API docs.
